sprint: Scene Compile Order Now Respects "Reorder Scenes" Instead of Raw Creation Order
task: A real, user-reported bug found from a side-by-side screenshot comparison: the World Builder v2 Scenes Library showed the authored/reordered order (First, Single Holder, Single Holder, second -- 'second' drag-reordered down to the end via Reorder Scenes), but Creator's Choose a Page Style showed a different order (First, second, Single Holder, Single Holder) -- 'second' back in its original creation-order position. Root cause: builder.js's convergeScenes() iterates projectLoader.getFilesInFolder('scenes'), which js/projectCompiler.js's loadIntoProjectLoader() builds from Object.keys(project.files) -- raw file-insertion (creation) order, completely bypassing project.sceneOrder (what Reorder Scenes' drag-and-drop actually writes to, and what ProjectModel.scenes(project) already correctly resolves everywhere else in the Builder UI). Fixed by having loadIntoProjectLoader() -- the one adapter boundary that still sees the full, live project object -- re-sort folders.scenes to match ProjectModel.scenes(project)'s own authored order immediately after building it from raw paths. Applied identically to both tools/world-builder-v2/ and its tools/world-builder/ (v1) twin, which carries the identical bug. Verified via a new Playwright reproduction: created four Scenes in creation order, called the real ProjectModel.moveScene() (the same function Reorder Scenes calls) to push one to the end, confirmed the compiled Representation order matched raw creation order (bug reproduced) before the fix and matched the reordered display after it, for both Builders. Full regression across the entire existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright suite and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged, zero console errors. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
